text: A wave of anxiety and self-blame hangs thick in the air, echoing in the repeated apologies I hear. The sterile, futuristic ambiance of this research space does little to comfort me; if anything, its glass and glowing lights seem to heighten my sense of exposure and vulnerability. Standing on the threshold of a room dedicated to innovation and inquiry, I feel deeply unsettled—torn between wanting to retreat and feeling obligated to stay and seek understanding. This moment is a collision of personal turmoil and impersonal technology, leaving me both curious and uneasy about my place here.
